retrieval_text: Rosemary is a culinary medicinal plant profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 315 works and 886 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Rosemary, Rosemary Flowers. Common plant-part co-mentions include Flower, Root, Leaf, Seed, Tops, Bark. Common preparation co-mentions include Oil, Tincture, Liniment, Powder, Bath, Extract. Common condition co-mentions include Cold or catarrh, Rheumatism, Wounds or ulcers, Cough, Colic, Vomiting or nausea. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Child-use caution, Toxicity watch, Fatal-risk warning, Pregnancy caution. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J.
